pubmed_links: [Fiorentino et al. (2015) Rare variation in SLC1A2 associated with bipolar disorder and schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25406999/); [Deng et al. (2004) Association study of polymorphisms in SLC1A2 with schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15296513/); [Xu et al. (2008) 11p14-p12 deletion in WAGR syndrome identifying genes contributing to autism](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19096215/)
summary: SLC1A2 encodes the glutamate transporter EAAT2, responsible for ~90% of synaptic glutamate clearance; rare coding variants have been associated with bipolar disorder and schizophrenia, and haploinsufficiency in WAGR syndrome correlates with autism. (well-established)
